passage.title: Healthy Start Check
passage.text: Our class tested two ways to feel ready after a long sitting time last month. Sora used taking a short rest at the desk, but Evan used easy arm and leg stretches. After a school week, Evan focused more easily because moving his arms and legs gave him fresh energy. The teacher checked how quickly each student started the next task. When Sora saw the difference, she changed to easy arm and leg stretches. After more practice, her body felt less tired during lessons. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that a simple stretch routine can help after long sitting.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Finding a better way to feel ready for work again
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Evan do after sitting for a long time?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does stretch mean in the second sentence?
choices: Move the body to loosen it
A person who visits a club
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Sora switch to easy arm and leg stretches?
choices: The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
She thought the stretches would give her more energy
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
